description: The CANONICAL Nimiq fleet baseline that `nq align` grades every app's nimiq-stack.json against. Derived from the proven shipping stack (SnapPOS RPC-settlement, nimiq-split reference manifest, TipJar/Beelink deploy kit). This file is the single committed source of truth for 'what a canonical chain app looks like'. Drift off these values is graded with the same vocabulary as nq audit: clean (matches), safe-drift (different but defensible — warn), risky-fail (load-bearing axis violated — fail).

miniApp.note: Every fleet chain app must run as a Nimiq Pay mini-app. CLEAN when the app imports nimiq-app-shell's createWallet OR references the real mini-app surface (@nimiq/mini-app-sdk, window.nimiq, window.nimiqPay). A competition-era STUB/MOCK provider (or nothing) is SAFE-DRIFT — advisory only, NEVER risky — so the axis tracks adoption without failing CI fleet-wide while apps wire to the real SDK. Only graded for chainApp / non-exempt repos.

i18n.note: Every fleet app ships runtime language switching from the shared shell. CLEAN when it uses nimiq-app-shell's createI18n OR vue-i18n OR carries a locales dir with >1 language file. Hardcoded-English is SAFE-DRIFT — advisory only, NEVER risky — so the axis tracks i18n adoption without failing CI. Only graded for chainApp.

deps.note: Shared fleet packages (nimiq-settlement, nimiq-app-shell) are consumed as pinned git deps. This axis flags (SAFE-DRIFT, advisory) a dependency pinned to an OLDER git tag than the latest published tag, resolved live via `git ls-remote --tags`. Network failures skip the check quietly (offline = clean). Never risky.

identity.note: The fleet was renamed to dotted `nimiq.<seg>` repo names, but code/env/manifests kept stale codenames (splitlink/tipjar/snappos/goalvault/gateflo…). The identity axis fails a repo whose internal name (package.json/nimiq-stack.json) or load-bearing identity files drift from the repo name. ONLY enforced when the canonical repo name matches /^nimiq\.[a-z0-9-]+$/ — a real fleet repo. Non-fleet dirs and test fixtures stay clean (not-applicable).

lightClient.note: forbiddenCalls is intentionally ONLY `Client.create(` (constructing the @nimiq/core light client). `waitForConsensusEstablished(` is NOT forbidden: the canonical rpc-block-scan client implements it as a NimiqClientLike interface method (RPC poll of isConsensusEstablished), so flagging it would punish the correct pattern.
lightClient.reason: The @nimiq/core light client never reaches consensus on our hosts (WASM addEventListener bug, hangs in Bun/Node through 2.7.0). Chain reads MUST use the rpc-block-scan path via the nimiq-settlement package; @nimiq/core is offline-crypto-only (key/tx signing).
